window.label: Entire history
window.phrase: the entire available history up to 2026-07-22

evidence.claim: The UAE Red Crescent completed construction on a second field hospital in eastern Chad near the Sudanese border on July 18, 2024.
evidence.snippet: The UAE Red Crescent finalized construction of an additional field hospital to provide medical aid to displaced Sudanese refugees.

evidence.claim: The UAE participated as an observer in the US-convened Sudan peace and ceasefire talks in Geneva on August 14, 2024.
evidence.snippet: UAE diplomats participated in peace talks in Geneva, advocating for immediate humanitarian access corridors across Sudan.

evidence.claim: On October 5, 2024, a UAE humanitarian vessel docked at Port Sudan carrying 1,200 tons of urgent medical and food supplies.
evidence.snippet: A large-scale UAE cargo shipment arrived in Port Sudan, delivering essential medical equipment and humanitarian food packages.

evidence.claim: Invictus Investment signed a logistics framework on December 1, 2024, to facilitate grain supply chains from stable agricultural zones in Sudan.
evidence.snippet: Invictus Investment partnered with local suppliers to coordinate secured agricultural supply chains and grain exports amidst ongoing challenges.

evidence.claim: The UAE committed USD 15 million in direct funding to UN-led humanitarian programs operating inside Sudan on January 28, 2025.
evidence.snippet: The UAE pledged USD 15 million to the United Nations Humanitarian Fund to assist displaced communities and combat food insecurity.

evidence.claim: The UAE Red Crescent launched a clean water program in eastern Sudan on March 12, 2025, installing 15 solar-powered purification wells.
evidence.snippet: To support regional refugee camps, the UAE funded the installation of 15 robust, solar-powered water purification wells.

evidence.claim: AD Ports Group held consultations on April 15, 2025, regarding the future layout of the Abu Amama Port and Economic Zone project.
evidence.snippet: AD Ports Group assessed preliminary plans for the proposed Red Sea port project, noting dependencies on regional security stability.

evidence.claim: The UAE Foreign Ministry issued a formal appeal on May 25, 2025, urging Sudanese factions to protect civilian infrastructure and reopen hospitals.
evidence.snippet: The UAE issued a diplomatic call for a permanent ceasefire, condemning targeted attacks on critical civilian utility networks.

evidence.claim: The Abu Dhabi Fund for Development (ADFD) announced a temporary deferment of Sudan's active debt servicing on June 18, 2025.
evidence.snippet: ADFD suspended active debt payments for Sudan to alleviate macroeconomic pressure during the severe internal humanitarian crisis.

evidence.claim: A joint UAE-UN emergency airbridge delivered 150 tons of therapeutic food to clinics in Darfur on July 10, 2025.
evidence.snippet: A joint humanitarian airbridge facilitated the direct transport of 150 tons of nutritional supplies to vulnerable children.

edges.claim: The UAE Red Crescent completed construction on a second field hospital in eastern Chad near the Sudanese border on July 18, 2024.
The UAE Foreign Ministry issued a formal appeal on May 25, 2025, urging Sudanese factions to protect civilian infrastructure and reopen hospitals.
AD Ports Group held consultations on April 15, 2025, regarding the future layout of the Abu Amama Port and Economic Zone project.
The Abu Dhabi Fund for Development (ADFD) announced a temporary deferment of Sudan's active debt servicing on June 18, 2025.
Co-mentioned in the same evidence record (E1) — plausible unstated relationship.
Co-mentioned in the same evidence record (E4) — plausible unstated relationship.
Co-mentioned in the same evidence record (E5) — plausible unstated relationship.
